title: Apple and pear pie
subtitle: An autumn double-crust pie with Bramley apple and Conference pear
excerpt: A British-leaning double-crust pie with the sharpness of Bramley apple balanced by the sweet, soft texture of Conference pear. Warm spice throughout. The Bramley collapses into the filling while the pear holds its shape.
sourceNotes: Apple pie has been a British staple since the fourteenth century. The addition of pear to the filling is a domestic British variation that softens the Bramley's sharp edge and adds a different texture. This recipe synthesises the double-crust pie method common to American baking (Joy of Cooking, Rombauer, 2006) with the British preference for Bramley cooking apple and Conference pear, two of the most widely available autumn fruits in UK supermarkets.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze unbaked assembled pie for up to 3 months. Bake from frozen, adding 20 minutes. Or freeze baked pie; reheat at 160°C for 20 minutes.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Pastry can be made 3 days ahead. Assemble and bake on the day.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; start at 200°C for 20 minutes, reduce to 180°C for remaining time
baking.decoratingTechnique: egg wash and demerara sugar scattered over the top crust

content.text: The Bramley and the Conference behave differently in the oven. The Bramley breaks down completely, giving body and juice to the filling. The Conference holds its shape and contributes a different, softer texture alongside the collapsed apple. Cut the pear larger than the apple: roughly 3 cm pieces will survive the baking while the Bramley dissolves around them.

The filling will shrink during baking. Mound it generously in the centre of the pastry base before covering: a flat filling produces a disappointingly sunken top crust. The fruit should be piled about 4 cm above the rim of the dish before the top crust goes on.

attrs.prepNote: peeled, cored, cut into 3 cm pieces
beaten with 1 tbsp milk, for egg wash

content.text: Rub the cold {{unsalted-butter}} into the {{plain-flour}}, {{caster-sugar}}, and {{fine-sea-salt}} until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Add 5 to 7 tablespoons of ice-cold water until the dough comes together. Divide into two discs, wrap, and refrigerate for 1 hour. Toss the chopped {{apple-bramley}} and {{pear-conference}} with the {{caster-sugar}}, {{cornflour}}, {{cinnamon-ground}}, {{mixed-spice}}, and {{lemon-juice}}. Roll the larger pastry disc and line the pie dish. Fill with the fruit mixture, mounded generously. Top with the second rolled disc, trim, crimp, and cut vents.

Heat the oven to 200°C fan. Brush the top crust with {{eggs}} wash and scatter {{demerara-sugar}} over. Bake for 20 minutes at 200°C, then reduce to 180°C for a further 30 to 35 minutes until deep golden and the filling is bubbling through the vents. Cool on a rack for at least 1 hour before cutting. Serve warm or at room temperature with cream or custard.
